sentence: Subjective:
The patient, William, presented with a history of a recent knee injury that occurred a week ago while participating in trampolining.
He reported hearing a snap and experiencing instant pain in his right knee.
The patient stated that this is the first time he has participated in trampolining and it was done for fun, not as part of a regular exercise routine.
He has tried taking Tylenol for the pain, but it has not provided significant relief.
The patient has been elevating the knee and has not used heat or ice.
He has a history of hypertension and diabetes, and has been taking lisinopril 20mg daily and metformin 500mg daily, but has not been consistently taking his medication.
The patient reports that his blood sugars have been slightly better, but he has not been measuring them regularly, and he is not on a specific diet.
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's blood pressure was 180/80.
Physical exam: The patient had a slight limp when walking, and there was ecchymosis and edema along the lateral aspect of the knee.
The knee had a decent range of motion, but the patient experienced pain with movement.
Test Results: The right knee x-ray showed no evidence of fracture or bony abnormality.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a lateral ligament strain in the right knee, hypertension, and diabetes.
Problem list: Right knee pain; hypertension; diabetes; suspected bony abnormality; unknown.
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 15mg daily for pain and swelling in the right knee.
I referred the patient to physical therapy to help strengthen the muscles around the knee and prevent further injury.
I will continue the lisinopril at 20mg daily for hypertension, and I will order an echo to assess the patient's cardiac function.
I will also order an A1c to evaluate the patient's long-term blood sugar control and a lipid panel to assess the patient's lipid profile.
Follow-up: I will see the patient again to discuss the results of the echo, A1c, and lipid panel, and to adjust the patient's medication as needed.
